channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Campaign text messages could soon get more effective — and annoying
item.description: Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages, making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.
item.content:encoded:
Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages, making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Maham Javaid

item.title: Shy on the dance floor? Virtual reality 'partners' aim to help you find your groove
item.description: VR dance lesson apps like Dance Guru and Trip the Light offer a judgment-free way to learn partner dancing.
item.content:encoded:
VR dance lesson apps like Dance Guru and Trip the Light offer a judgment-free way to learn partner dancing.

(Image credit: Chloe Veltman)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Waymo called the cops on teen riders, raising privacy concerns
item.description: Two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from a driverless taxi when the company disabled it and alerted police.
item.content:encoded:
Two 15-year-olds were allegedly drinking alcohol and shooting toy guns from a driverless taxi when the company disabled it and alerted police.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Class action suit against AI makers over deepfake child sexual abuse material expands
item.description: New plaintiffs in a lawsuit against Elon Musk's SpaceXAI and Stability AI say the companies' AI tools were used to make sexually explicit images of them as children.
item.content:encoded:
New plaintiffs in a lawsuit against Elon Musk's SpaceXAI and Stability AI say the companies' AI tools were used to make sexually explicit images of them as children.

(Image credit: Pablo Vera)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Campaign staffers keep trying to bet on races despite push to curb insider trading
item.description: Kalshi says it has blocked "dozens" of trades from campaign insiders, but experts say the company's approach leaves lots of potential loopholes. NPR has found at least one trade that slipped through.
item.content:encoded:
Kalshi says it has blocked "dozens" of trades from campaign insiders, but experts say the company's approach leaves lots of potential loopholes. NPR has found at least one trade that slipped through.

(Image credit: Allison Robbert)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: How South Korea's silicon belt is changing its society
item.description: The global race for AI chips is minting a new elite in South Korea -- and raising questions about who gets left behind.
item.content:encoded: The global race for AI chips is minting a new elite in South Korea -- and raising questions about who gets left behind.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Why AI companies are hiring philosophers to help develop their models
item.description: A growing number of AI labs have been hiring from a surprising pool of candidates: philosophers. NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Benjamin Sutherland, who recently wrote about this for The Economist.
item.content:encoded: A growing number of AI labs have been hiring from a surprising pool of candidates: philosophers. NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Benjamin Sutherland, who recently wrote about this for The Economist.
item.dc:creator: Linah Mohammad

item.title: Supreme Court lets Texas restrict minors' access to app stores for the time being
item.description: Texas' App Store Accountability Act requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps. The Supreme Court says the law can go into effect as lawsuits continue in lower courts.
item.content:encoded:
Texas' App Store Accountability Act requires minors to have their parents' permission to download most apps. The Supreme Court says the law can go into effect as lawsuits continue in lower courts.

(Image credit: Paul Ellis)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: A hot summer trend in the sharing economy? Rental swimming pools
item.description: The Airbnb-style company Swimply said there have been about 275,000 private pool reservations so far this year.
item.content:encoded:
The Airbnb-style company Swimply said there have been about 275,000 private pool reservations so far this year.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Meta considered buying Kalshi before developing its own prediction market app
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg met with Kalshi's CEO last year about a potential deal, but talks did not move forward. Now Meta is making its own prediction market app.
item.content:encoded:
Mark Zuckerberg met with Kalshi's CEO last year about a potential deal, but talks did not move forward. Now Meta is making its own prediction market app.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon/AFP and Aaron Schwartz/Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn
